extraction.one_line_summary: MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe attacked from breakaway on Col de Turini, dropped YATES Simon with LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel cooperating, won stage while BERNAL Egan regained yellow by limiting losses.
tactical_patterns: MARTÍNEZ and LÓPEZ cooperated to neutralize YATES despite competing for stage win, revealing tactical maturity in breakaway scenarios.
Sky allowed 39-rider breakaway containing ŠIŠKEVIČIUS at 2:01 GC deficit, demonstrating controlled risk tolerance with Turini finish ahead.
KWIATKOWSKI dropped from podium to 4th defending BERNAL, exposing Sky's willingness to sacrifice domestique GC for leader protection.
YATES Simon isolated when MARTÍNEZ and LÓPEZ paired against him, showing weakness when outnumbered by coordinated climbers.

rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: positive: 22-year-old won biggest career victory by tactically isolating stronger-positioned YATES through Colombian cooperation
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: sacrificed stage win chance to work against YATES, then still finished 2nd demonstrating tactical intelligence
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: recovered yellow jersey with 45-second margin despite not featuring in stage action, revealing underlying strength
rider_signals.ŠIŠKEVIČIUS Evaldas: neutral: highest-placed breakaway rider at 2:01 deficit, used as Sky's risk calculation threshold for allowing escape
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: negative: dropped from podium to 4th at 46 seconds while defending BERNAL, confirming domestique role over personal GC
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: isolated and dropped by MARTÍNEZ/LÓPEZ cooperation despite initiating decisive attack, vulnerable when outnumbered
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: negative: 2009 winner dropped on Turini climb, confirming age-related decline on sustained steep gradients
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: unable to follow BERNAL's pace on Turini, exposing gap to elite climbers on decisive summit finishes
